content: Combining Self-Refine's unsupervised iterative self-feedback framework with LLM self-reflection mechanisms leads to the novel hypothesis that multi-perspective inconsistency (a core insight from modern self-reflection research) can be embedded directly into Self-Refine's iterative loop to improve output quality without adding training or external supervision. This creates a hybrid framework that uses conflicting self-generated perspectives as the feedback trigger for each refinement step, rather than relying on generic self-criticism of a single output.
